_note: English translations of finding messages. Each key parallels uk.json — same {var} placeholders, same explanatory tone (terse + educational). Adtech-native English: keep terms like seatbid/adomain/bidfloor/imp/IAB/oRTB/VAST as-is rather than over-localising. {{macro}} renders as literal {macro} so creative-macro names stay readable.
request.id_required: BidRequest has no id. Without it the SSP can't match its log to the bid — add a non-empty BidRequest.id (any unique string within the auction).
request.imp_required: Empty imp[]. A request with no slot is an empty auction note — the bidder has nothing to bid on. Add at least one impression object.
request.no_site_or_app: Neither site nor app is set. The bidder needs to know where the creative will render (web vs in-app) — fill exactly one of site / app.
request.site_and_app_both: Both site and app are set. Per oRTB §3.2.1 a BidRequest must carry exactly one — some SSPs reject the request outright, others silently pick one. Remove whichever object doesn't apply.
request.at_required: Missing auction type (at). Required per oRTB §3.2.1 — set 1 (first-price, common for header bidding) or 2 (second-price, classic Vickrey). Without it bidders can't price correctly and many SSPs reject the request outright.
request.at_invalid: Auction type at = {at} is non-standard. Allowed: 1 (first-price, typical for CPC) or 2 (second-price). Other values won't be accepted by spec-compliant SSPs.
regs.gdpr_consent_missing: regs.ext.gdpr=1 (GDPR applies) but user.ext.consent is empty. EU exchanges drop the bid without a TCF v2 consent string. Add user.ext.consent or set regs.ext.gdpr=0 if the request is outside EU scope.
imp.bidfloorcur_missing: Slot #{num}: bidfloor is set but bidfloorcur is missing. Per oRTB §3.2.4 always pair them (e.g. bidfloorcur: "USD") — defaults vary by exchange and the bid may be misinterpreted.
request.device_required: No device block. Without it the user device can't be identified — the bidder loses targeting + anti-fraud signal. Add device.{ua, ip, ...}.
request.device.ip_required: device has neither ip nor ipv6. Geo-targeting and fraud detection rely on the address — without it the bidder will likely no-bid.
request.device.ua_required: device.ua (User-Agent) is missing. Without it browser/OS/mobile detection breaks — the bidder can't match the inventory to a creative.
request.device.geo.country_invalid: Country "{country}" is not in ISO-3166 alpha-3 format. Three uppercase letters expected: "USA", "GBR", "UKR". Two-letter codes ("US", "GB") aren't accepted here.
request.device.language_invalid: Language "{language}" is not in ISO-639 alpha-2 format. Expected "uk", "en", or with a region tag like "en-US". Three-letter codes don't fit this field.
request.device.language_missing: device.language is unset. Not critical, but the user's language is a useful targeting signal — bidders may filter accordingly.
request.user.gender_invalid: user.gender = "{gender}" — invalid. Per spec only M (male), F (female), O (other) are allowed. Other values will be ignored or rejected.
request.site.domain_missing: site.domain is unset. This is the domain-level taxonomy hint — without it SSPs can't enforce category filters and bidders lose context.
request.app.bundle_missing: app.bundle is unset. Without a bundle ID (e.g. "com.example.app") the app can't be uniquely identified — store-listing match breaks.
request.bcat_invalid: bcat must be an array of IAB category strings (e.g. ["IAB7-39","IAB25"]). With the wrong type SSPs will skip blocked-category enforcement.
imp.id_required: Slot #{num}: missing imp.id. Without it the bid can't be tied back to a specific slot — the SSP will reject the response.
imp.bidfloor_invalid: Slot #{num}: bidfloor must be a number (e.g. 0.05). It's another type now, so the auction can't determine whether bids clear the floor.
imp.format_required: Slot #{num}: no creative format specified. Add at least one of: banner, video, native, audio. Without it the bidder doesn't know what format to bid.
imp.banner.size_required: Slot #{num}: banner has no dimensions. Specify w and h (e.g. 300×250) or a format[] array of allowed sizes.
imp.video.mimes_required: Slot #{num}: video has no video.mimes. List the MIME types the player accepts (e.g. ["video/mp4","video/webm"]). Without it the bidder doesn't know what to send.
imp.video.protocols_missing: Slot #{num}: consider adding video.protocols — the list of VAST versions accepted (e.g. [2,3,7]). Without it the bidder defaults conservatively.
imp.video.protocols_unknown: Slot #{num}: video.protocols contains unknown codes {values}. Allowed: 1-14 (VAST 1.0 through 4.2 wrappers) or 500+ for exchange-specific. Anything else is malformed.
imp.native.assets_required: Slot #{num}: native request has no assets[]. Native 1.1 requires the asset list (title, image, data...). Without it the bidder can't render anything.
imp.native.ver_missing: Slot #{num}: native.ver is unset. Recommended "1.1" or "1.2" so the bidder knows which spec to follow when shaping the response.
imp.native.invalid_json: Slot #{num}: native.request is not valid JSON ({error}). This field must be a JSON-encoded native request (string or object) — fix the encoding.
response.id_required: Response has no id. It must be the same as BidRequest.id — otherwise the SSP can't match the response to the request.
response.seatbid_required: No seatbid[]. This is the array of seat bids — without it the response simply has no bids. Equivalent to no-bid (HTTP 204).
response.seatbid.empty: Seatbid #{num}: empty bid[]. A seat bid with no bids is equivalent to a "no bid" — drop the empty seatbid or fill it.
response.bid.id_required: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: missing bid.id. This is the unique bid identifier used in nurl/burl callbacks — required.
response.bid.impid_required: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: missing bid.impid. Must reference a specific imp.id from the request — without it the bid can't be tied to a slot.
response.bid.price_required: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: bid.price is missing or not a number. This is the bid amount — without it the auction is meaningless.
response.bid.payload_missing: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: neither adm nor nurl is set. One of them is required: adm — inline creative, nurl — win-notice URL with the creative.
response.bid.adomain_missing: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: missing adomain[]. This is the advertiser-domain list — required for badv-blocking and brand-safety checks.
extrtb.ext.bsection_invalid: ext.bsection — vendor extension for section blocking. Must be an array of integers, otherwise it is ignored and the block has no effect.
extrtb.ext.btags_invalid: ext.btags — vendor extension for tag blocking. Must be an array of integers, otherwise it is ignored and the block has no effect.
extrtb.imp.subage_missing: Slot #{num}: ext.subage (subscription age in days) is missing for a push impression. Without it the SSP treats the subscriber as 'unknown' and reduces the bid — add a number.
extrtb.push_detected: Push traffic detected. To maximise buyout, populate subage / subage0 / subage_dt — the SSP uses them to score subscriber quality.
extrtb.bid.macro_unsupported: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: macro ${{macro}} is not substituted by this vendor. Only three are supported: AUCTION_PRICE, AUCTION_CURRENCY, AUCTION_LOSS — others remain as literal strings in the creative.
inpage-push.title_required: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: In-Page Push bid without `bid.ext.title` (or alias: text). Without a headline the publisher renders an empty card — impression is lost.
inpage-push.title_too_long: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: headline is {len} characters (recommended max {max}). Most In-Page Push templates truncate above {max} — copy beyond that boundary is invisible.
inpage-push.image_required: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: In-Page Push bid without `bid.ext.image` (or alias: image_url, picture). The hero image is the visual hook; without it the format collapses to text-only.
inpage-push.image_invalid_url: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: image URL `{url}…` is not http(s). The asset will not load on any publisher origin.
inpage-push.click_required: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: In-Page Push bid without `bid.ext.url` (or alias: click, click_url, href, link). No click URL means no monetisation — every click goes nowhere.
inpage-push.click_invalid_url: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: click URL `{url}…` is not http(s). Browsers reject non-http(s) navigations from sandboxed iframes — every click silently dies.
inpage-push.icon_invalid_url: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: icon URL `{url}…` is not http(s). The card falls back to a no-icon layout instead of the branded variant.
inpage-push.desc_too_long: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: description is {len} characters (recommended max {max}). Most templates truncate above {max}.
inpage-push.cta_too_long: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: CTA button text is {len} characters (recommended max {max}). Buttons clip on mobile after ~{max}.
feed.shape_unknown: Doesn't match any known JSON-feed shape — vendor push array, `{result: {listing: [...]}}` clickunder wrapper, or a single-bid vendor object. Check the response wrapper structure.
feed.clickunder.url_required: Click-listing #{num}: missing url. This is the click target — without a URL the clickunder has nothing to redirect to.
feed.clickunder.bid_required: Click-listing #{num}: bid must be a number (CPC). Without it the auction can't determine the winning price.
feed.push.id_required: Push material #{num}: missing id. Unique identifier of the material — used in subsequent click/win callbacks.
feed.push.click_url_required: Push material #{num}: neither click_url nor link is set. Click destination is required — without it the push notification has no action.
feed.push.bid_required: Push material #{num}: missing cpc / price. A bid (number) per click or impression is required — without it the auction has no signal.
feed.push.bid_string_type: Push material #{num}: cpc is a string ('{val}'), but the spec wants a number. Most SSPs parseFloat it and let it through; strict parsers will reject.
feed.push.bid_not_numeric: Push material #{num}: cpc / price is present but doesn't parse as a number. The bidder can't compute economics.
feed.push.title_recommended: Push material #{num}: title is recommended — the headline shown in the push notification. Without it the user sees a generic "New message" prompt.
feed.push.image_url_recommended: Push material #{num}: image_url is recommended — a large image lifts CTR significantly on push surfaces.
feed.push.nurl_recommended: Push material #{num}: nurl is missing (and not embedded in icon_url) — win notification won't fire, billing tracking breaks.
feed.valuefeed.id_required: No `id` field — the bid identifier returned to the initiator for win/loss tracking.
feed.valuefeed.value_required: No `value` (number) — this is the bid price (CPM) for the auction.
feed.valuefeed.click_url_required: No `clickUrl` — the destination when the user clicks. Without it the bid cannot deliver.
feed.valuefeed.title_recommended: No `title` set — a push/native creative without a headline renders as a generic prompt.
feed.valuefeed.description_recommended: No `description` set — for native/push this supplements the title.
feed.valuefeed.notify_recommended: Neither `iconUrl` nor `nUrl` is set — win notification will not fire and the creative has no thumbnail.
feed.bidprice.bid_price_required: No `bid_price` (number) — this is the bid amount in the auction.
feed.bidprice.link_required: No `link` — the final destination URL after click. Without it the bid cannot convert.
feed.bidprice.notification_url_recommend: No `notification_url` set — the SSP uses this to confirm impression delivery.
feed.bidprice.copy_recommended: Neither `title` nor `message` is set — the user-facing text is empty.
feed.bidprice.icon_recommended: No `icon` set for push/mini-app — icon presence typically lifts CTR.
feed.bidredirect.bid_required: No `bid` (number) — the auction price for the redirect slot.
feed.bidredirect.redirecturl_required: No `redirecturl` — where to redirect the user after a win.
feed.bidredirect.campaignid_invalid: `campaignid` is present but is neither a string nor a number — this feed format expects a string ID.
payload.unknown_type: Couldn't determine payload type. Expected oRTB BidRequest (with imp[]), BidResponse (with seatbid[]), or a JSON feed (vendor push array, clickunder wrapper, or single-bid object). Check the root structure.
payload.invalid_root: Expected a JSON object or array. Other types (string, number) are not valid RTB payloads — paste the full request/response body.
crosscheck.no_request: No valid BidRequest to crosscheck against. Paste the request in the left field to compare it against the response.
crosscheck.no_response: Response has no seatbid[] or it's empty — nothing to crosscheck. Paste the BidResponse with at least one seat bid.
crosscheck.id_match: Request and response ids match ("{id}") — the SSP can pair them up.
crosscheck.id_mismatch: ids don't match: request "{reqId}", response "{resId}". The SSP will drop the bid — id pairing is the auction primary key.
crosscheck.cur_allowed: Currency "{cur}" is within the request's allowed list — fine.
crosscheck.cur_not_in_request: Response currency "{cur}" is not in the request's allowed list {allowed}. The SSP may convert (with a fee) or reject the bid.
crosscheck.bid.impid_resolved: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: impid "{impid}" resolved to slot imp.id "{impid}".
crosscheck.bid.impid_unresolved: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: impid "{impid}" doesn't match any imp.id in the request. The SSP will drop the bid.
crosscheck.bid.above_floor: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: price {price} ≥ floor {floor} — bid clears.
crosscheck.bid.below_floor: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: price {price} is below floor {floor} — bid will be rejected.
crosscheck.bid.floor_currency_mismatch: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: bid settles in {bidCur} but the floor is set in {floorCur} — price {price} can't be compared to floor {floor} without an FX rate. Floor check skipped.
crosscheck.bid.cat_clean: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: none of bid.cat overlap the request's bcat — bid clears category filter.
crosscheck.bid.cat_blocked: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: categories {categories} are in the request's bcat blocklist. The SSP will drop the bid.
crosscheck.bid.adomain_blocked: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: advertiser domain {domains} is in the request's badv blocklist. The SSP will drop the bid.
crosscheck.bid.size_match: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: creative size {w}×{h} fits the slot.
crosscheck.bid.size_mismatch: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: creative size {w}×{h} doesn't match any of the slot's allowed formats — the SSP may drop or rescale.
crosscheck.bid.native_complete: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: all {count} required native assets present.
crosscheck.bid.native_missing_assets: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: native response is missing required assets (id: {missing}). The SSP will drop the bid.
crosscheck.bid.native_extra_assets: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: native response has unexpected assets (id: {extra}) not declared in the request. May render fine but it's a contract violation.
crosscheck.bid.native_invalid_request: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: imp.native.request is not valid JSON. This field must be JSON-encoded native — fix the encoding before crosschecking.
crosscheck.bid.native_invalid_adm: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: bid.adm is not valid JSON for a native response. Native adm must be JSON-encoded `{native: {assets: [...]}}`.
crosscheck.bid.video_vast: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: adm is valid VAST XML for the video slot.
crosscheck.bid.video_not_vast: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: video slot, but adm doesn't look like VAST XML. Player may fail to play the creative.
crosscheck.auction.summary: Auction summary: {totalBids} bids · {bidsAboveFloor} clear floor · {impsFilled}/{impsTotal} slots filled.
imp.non_standard_format: Detected non-IAB format `{format}` at path `{path}`. Not in canonical oRTB — vendor-specific (pop / clickunder / pushunder / push).
info.adkernel.routed: Traffic is routed through AdKernel (alias `{alias}` at `{signal}`). AdKernel is a white-label engine powering 49+ alias networks (Waardex, Monetix, Denakop, Türk Telekom, Display.io, …). Wire format is standard oRTB 2.5 — tenant is identified by `host` + `zoneId`.
behavior.trap.invisible_overlay: Click landed on a transparent <{tagName}> covering {coverage} of the viewport (opacity {opacity}, bg-alpha {bgAlpha}). This is a click-skim trap — an invisible overlay above the creative captures incidental clicks. No legitimate creative ships this.
behavior.trap.invisible_overlay_aggregat: Creative contains {contributors} invisible clickable elements whose combined viewport coverage is {coverage} — a split click trap. Each element stays under the per-element threshold, but together they blanket the viewport. No legitimate creative needs this layout.
behavior.bot.center_synth: Click is script-synthesized: clientX/Y land at the geometric center of <{tagName}> (distance {distancePx}px) AND event.isTrusted = false. Real users don't hit pixel-perfect centers, and an isolated creative can't get isTrusted=true without WebDriver/CDP access. Canonical pixelbot signature.
behavior.bot.click_burst: {clickCount} clicks fired within {windowMs}ms on a single target. Humans don't tap that fast — looks like automated CTA / tracker abuse.
behavior.bot.phantom_click: Click on <{tagName}> (isTrusted={isTrusted}) fired with zero mousemove and zero touchstart since the creative loaded. Either the user clicked with no intervening motion (rare — happens if the cursor already sat over the iframe) or it's a synthetic click from a headless browser / pixelbot.
behavior.malicious.frame_bust_anchor: Click on <a target="{target}" href="{url}"> — attempt to escape the iframe via top-level navigation. Gesture grace: {withinGestureGrace} (msSinceGesture={msSinceGesture}). Some legitimate banners do link out via _top, but without a preceding user gesture this is a textbook frame-bust.
behavior.malicious.frame_bust_form: Form submission with target="{target}" → {url} (method: {method}). Forms inside a banner creative with target=_top have no legitimate use — this is either credential phishing or a top-frame redirect dressed up as something else.
behavior.malicious.auto_redirect: Auto-navigation without a user gesture: {method} → {url}. msSinceGesture={msSinceGesture}, userActivationEverActive={userActivationEverActive}. The creative is trying to navigate the user without input — the sandbox blocks the actual navigation, but the intent is recorded.
behavior.malicious.late_redirect: Delayed redirect: {method} → {url} fired {msSinceGesture}ms after the last gesture, but with an empty event-stack. Classic cloaking pattern — user clicked something visible, then setTimeout ran the real navigation outside the gesture lineage.
behavior.malicious.heavy_ad_cpu: Creative breached Chrome Heavy Ad Intervention CPU thresholds ({breachedThreshold}): {cumulativeSec}s cumulative blocking, {windowSec}s in the current 30s window. Limits: 60s cumulative or 4s in any 30s window. No legitimate banner needs that much CPU — likely a miner, runaway loop, or aggressive animation/tracking.
behavior.malicious.heavy_ad_network: Creative loaded {cumulativeMb} MB ({cumulativeBytes} bytes) across {resourceCount} resources — IAB/Chrome 4 MB cap breached. Banner creatives over 4 MB break mobile data plans and ruin LCP regardless of intent.
behavior.malicious.frozen_thread: Creative is frozen: the parent watchdog has not received a probe heartbeat for {secSinceLastHeartbeat}s (threshold 3.5s). The iframe's JS thread is blocked — infinite loop, sync XHR, or a script bug. Detection method: {method}.
behavior.malicious.permission_abuse: Creative invoked {method} (apiKind={apiKind}, mediaSubKind={mediaSubKind}). withinGestureGrace={withinGestureGrace}, msSinceGesture={msSinceGesture}. No banner has a legitimate reason to request push / geolocation / camera / fullscreen / SW permissions — without gesture lineage this is malware/spam; even with a gesture the creative is piggy-backing a system prompt onto a click the user didn't intend for that purpose.
behavior.static.obfuscation: Creative source contains obfuscation pattern `{pattern}` — eval(atob/decodeURIComponent), Function constructor, Dean Edwards packer, jjencode/aaencode signature. Legitimate banners ship readable HTML/JS; static obfuscation almost always hides a redirector, miner, or tracking primitive that the runtime probe might never observe in the dispatched event stream.
behavior.static.miner_signature: Creative source matches cryptocurrency miner signature `{signature}` (CoinHive, CryptoLoot, CryptoNight, JSEcoin, WebMinerPool, Monero Ocean, DeepMiner). No legitimate banner runs CPU mining — categorical malware regardless of whether the runtime probe observed CPU exhaustion this session.
behavior.static.xss_marker: Creative source contains DOM-XSS sink `{pattern}` — document.write/innerHTML fed by decodeURIComponent/unescape/atob, or setAttribute('on…') dynamic event injection. These shapes have no legitimate use in display ads; they're cloaked-redirect or credential-phishing primitives.
behavior.static.high_entropy_blob: Creative contains a high-entropy base64 blob ({blobLength} chars, entropy {blobEntropy} bits/char). Preview: `{blobPreview}…`. Could be a legitimate compressed asset (sprite/font) or a packed payload hiding code; surface for review.
crosscheck.cur_default_usd_mismatch: Response omits cur, which oRTB §3.3 defaults to USD — but the request's allowed currencies are {allowed}, with no USD entry. The SSP will read this as a currency mismatch and likely reject the bid.
crosscheck.bid.price_invalid: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: bid.price is invalid (raw value: {raw}). oRTB §3.2.5 requires bid.price as a positive number. The bid is structurally broken and won't auction-clear; auction summary excludes it.
response.seatbid_or_nbr_required: BidResponse has neither seatbid[] nor nbr. oRTB §3.3 says you must have one of: an array of seat bids, or a no-bid reason code. The exchange will treat this as malformed and drop the response.
response.no_bid: No-bid response (nbr={nbr}, seatbid empty/missing). This is the canonical "thanks for the request, but I'm not bidding" shape per oRTB §3.3.1. The nbr code tells the exchange WHY (e.g. 2 = invalid request, 4 = unknown ad position). Not an error.
response.seatbid_empty_no_nbr: seatbid is an empty array AND nbr is absent. oRTB §3.3.1 says: signal a no-bid via the nbr code, not by an empty seatbid. The exchange may reject this as malformed. Either fill seatbid or set nbr.
regs.gpp_sid_without_string: regs.gpp_sid lists section IDs but regs.gpp consent string is missing. The IDs reference sections of a string the exchange can't read. Either remove gpp_sid or add the matching gpp string.
regs.gpp_string_without_sid: regs.gpp consent string present but regs.gpp_sid is missing. Without the section IDs, the exchange can't tell which jurisdictions this consent applies to. Add gpp_sid (e.g. [7] for US-National, [8] for US-CA).
regs.us_privacy_invalid: regs.ext.us_privacy = "{usp}" is malformed. CCPA spec requires exactly 4 chars: spec_version (1-9) + opt_out_notice (-/Y/N) + opt_out (-/Y/N) + lspa_covered (-/Y/N). Common shape: "1YNN" (CCPA covered, opt-out signal not given, no LSPA).
regs.coppa_pii_present: regs.coppa=1 (user is a child under 13) but the request still carries PII (hasUid={hasUid}, hasGeo={hasGeo}). COPPA prohibits collecting personal information from children. Strip user.id/buyeruid and precise geo before sending — or set coppa=0 if the request isn't actually for a child.
vast.version_missing: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: VAST XML has no version attribute on the <VAST> element. IAB VAST spec requires a version (e.g. version="4.2"). Players use it to pick rendering rules — without it many players reject the response.
vast.version_unknown: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: VAST version="{ver}" is not a recognized major. VAST 1.x is dead, 5+ doesn't exist. Use 2.x, 3.x, or 4.x.
vast.inline_or_wrapper_required: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: VAST has neither <InLine> nor <Wrapper>. Each <Ad> must declare exactly one — InLine carries the creative directly, Wrapper points to another VAST URL.
vast.adsystem_missing: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: <InLine> is missing the required <AdSystem>. VAST §3.2 — the system that served the ad must identify itself. SSPs reject InLine ads without AdSystem.
vast.adtitle_missing: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: <InLine> is missing the required <AdTitle>. VAST §3.2 makes it mandatory — even an empty <AdTitle/> is fine, but it can't be absent.
vast.mediafile_missing: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: <InLine> has no <MediaFile>. Without media there is nothing to play. Add at least one <MediaFile> with URL, type, width, height.
vast.wrapper_no_tag_uri: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: <Wrapper> is missing <VASTAdTagURI>. The whole point of a Wrapper is to forward to another VAST tag — without the URL it does nothing.
vast.insecure_url: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: VAST contains {count} insecure http:// URL(s) in tracking/media tags (sample: {sampleUrl}). Modern publisher pages run on https — browsers block mixed content silently. Switch to https://.
request.30.envelope_required: oRTB 3.0 BidRequest is missing the openrtb envelope. 3.0 wraps the request as { openrtb: { ver, request: {...} } }; without this envelope the bidder can't even identify the protocol version. Add the wrapper or send 2.x shape.
request.30.ver_required: openrtb.ver is missing. 3.0 requires an explicit version string (e.g. "3.0"). Bidders use it to pick the right parsing path.
request.30.ver_invalid: openrtb.ver = "{ver}" is not a 3.x version. The 3.0 envelope only accepts versions starting with "3.". 2.x payloads should not use the envelope at all — send them flat.
request.30.request_required: openrtb.request object is missing. The 3.0 envelope has two children: ver + request. Without request there is nothing to bid on.
request.30.id_required: openrtb.request.id is missing. Same role as 2.x BidRequest.id — uniquely identifies the auction. Add a non-empty string.
request.30.item_required: openrtb.request.item[] is missing or empty. 3.0's item[] replaces 2.x imp[]: it lists the placements being auctioned. A request without items is an empty auction note.
request.30.context_recommended: openrtb.request.context is missing. Context carries site/app/device/regs/user (the flat 2.x fields moved here). Bidders need it to qualify the audience — without context most will skip the auction.
request.30.item.invalid: Item #{num} is not an object. Each entry in item[] must be { id, qty?, spec }.
request.30.item.id_required: Item #{num}: id is missing. Per-item id ties bid responses back to the right placement.
request.30.item.qty_invalid: Item #{num}: qty = {qty} is not a positive number. Spec defaults qty to 1 — leave it absent OR set a positive integer (the number of impressions this item represents, e.g. for ad pods).
request.30.item.spec_required: Item #{num}: spec is missing. spec describes WHAT this item is — placement type, dimensions, video / banner / native specifics per AdCOM 1.0. Without it bidders have no idea what creative shape to send.
request.30.deep_validation_limited: Note: oRTB 3.0 deep validation (AdCOM placement specs, full context targetings, GDPR/COPPA/CCPA/GPP compliance, media formats) is active.
request.30.context.site_and_app_both: openrtb.request.context: more than one distribution channel ({channels}) present. AdCOM site, app and dooh are mutually exclusive — keep exactly one.
request.30.context.no_site_or_app: openrtb.request.context: no distribution channel present. AdCOM expects exactly one of site, app or dooh to qualify the inventory context.
request.30.context.site_invalid: openrtb.request.context.site must be a valid JSON object.
request.30.context.site.domain_missing: openrtb.request.context.site.domain is missing or not a string. Bidders need the domain to filter against advertiser block-lists.
request.30.context.app_invalid: openrtb.request.context.app must be a valid JSON object.
request.30.context.app.bundle_missing: openrtb.request.context.app.bundle is missing or not a string. DSPs require the application's unique bundle identifier for precise targeting.
request.30.context.device_required: openrtb.request.context.device is missing. Target device characteristics (IP, UA) are required for downstream qualification and fraud detection.
request.30.context.device_invalid: openrtb.request.context.device must be a valid JSON object.
request.30.context.device.ip_required: openrtb.request.context.device: ip or ipv6 is missing. Bidders require the client's IP address for geo-targeting and frequency capping.
request.30.context.device.ua_required: openrtb.request.context.device.ua is missing or not a string. The User Agent is required to parse hardware, platform, and browser types.
request.30.context.device.geo_invalid: openrtb.request.context.device.geo must be a valid JSON object.
request.30.context.device.geo.country_in: openrtb.request.context.device.geo.country = "{country}" is not a valid ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code. Must be a 3-letter uppercase country code (e.g. "USA", "UKR").
request.30.context.device.language_inval: openrtb.request.context.device.lang = "{language}" is not a valid ISO 639-1 alpha-2 code. Must be a 2-letter lowercase language code (e.g. "en", "uk").
request.30.context.device.language_missi: openrtb.request.context.device.lang is missing. Bidders use the device language to pick localized creatives. Spec recommends specifying it.
request.30.regs_invalid: openrtb.request.context.regs must be a valid JSON object.
request.30.regs.gdpr_consent_missing: GDPR is enabled, but openrtb.request.context.user.consent (or ext.consent) is missing or empty. Bidders cannot legally serve personalized ads under GDPR without a consent string.
request.30.regs.coppa_pii_present: COPPA is enabled (coppa=1), but PII targeting details ({hasUid} uid / {hasGeo} geo coordinates) are present. To comply with COPPA, precise geo and user-level identifiers must be stripped.
request.30.regs.us_privacy_invalid: openrtb.request.context.regs.ext.us_privacy = "{usp}" is invalid. IAB CCPA / MSPA us_privacy must be a 4-character string of version + notices + opt-out (e.g. "1YYN", "1---").
request.30.regs.gpp_sid_without_string: GPP section IDs are declared (gpp_sid), but the main GPP consent string (gpp) is missing. The GPP framework requires both to be populated.
request.30.regs.gpp_string_without_sid: GPP consent string (gpp) is present, but GPP section IDs (gpp_sid) are missing or empty. Downstream bidders will not know which privacy guidelines to apply.
request.30.context.user_invalid: openrtb.request.context.user must be a valid JSON object.
request.30.context.user.gender_invalid: openrtb.request.context.user.gender = "{gender}" is invalid. Must be M (Male), F (Female), or O (Other).
request.30.item.placement_required: Item #{num}: spec.placement is missing. AdCOM requires placement specificities to define the creative format.
request.30.item.placement_invalid: Item #{num}: spec.placement must be a valid JSON object.
request.30.item.placement_format_require: Item #{num}: spec.placement has no format details. At least one format of display, video, audio, or native must be defined.
request.30.item.display_invalid: Item #{num}: spec.placement.display must be a valid JSON object.
request.30.item.display.size_invalid: Item #{num}: spec.placement.display has invalid dimensions (width/height must be positive numbers).
request.30.item.video_invalid: Item #{num}: spec.placement.video must be a valid JSON object.
request.30.item.video.mime_required: Item #{num}: spec.placement.video.mime must be a non-empty array of strings representing supported video MIME types.
request.30.item.video.ctype_recommended: Item #{num}: spec.placement.video.ctype is recommended — declare accepted creative subtypes (VAST versions) per AdCOM List: Creative Subtypes — Audio/Video. AdCOM 3.0 uses ctype here, not the 2.x protocols field.
request.30.item.video.dur_invalid: Item #{num}: spec.placement.video.maxdur has invalid duration constraints (mindur={mindur}, maxdur={maxdur}). Duration bounds must be positive numbers and mindur <= maxdur.
request.30.item.audio_invalid: Item #{num}: spec.placement.audio must be a valid JSON object.
request.30.item.audio.mime_required: Item #{num}: spec.placement.audio.mime must be a non-empty array of strings representing supported audio MIME types.
request.30.item.native_invalid: Item #{num}: spec.placement.native must be a valid JSON object.
version.mismatch: Declared oRTB version "{expected}" but field-presence detection landed on "{detected}" (confidence {confidence}) — triggered by {signals}. Either the payload contains version-{detected}-only fields that don't belong in a {expected} request (remove them if you're pinning to {expected}), or opts.expectedVersion is stale and the traffic has already moved to {detected} (update the pin).
vast.ad_pod: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: VAST contains {count} <Ad> elements (an ad-pod — sequential video ads, e.g. a pre-roll cluster). Spec-valid; not every player handles ad-pods, and downstream tracker fires can need adjustment.
vast.linear_duration_missing: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: <Linear> element is present but <Duration> is missing. VAST §3.7 — linear duration is mandatory; without it players can't tell when the ad ends and tracker fires get unreliable.
vast.vpaid_deprecated: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: VAST creative declares apiFramework="VPAID". VPAID was deprecated in VAST 4.1 and REMOVED in 4.2 — most modern SSPs flag it as legacy / risk. Use OMID for viewability instead.
vast.impression_tracking_missing: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: <InLine> has no <Impression> tracking tag. Spec recommends at least one Impression beacon — without it the publisher and DSP can't reconcile delivery. (WARN, not ERROR — some setups fire impressions server-side, but it's still suspicious.)
vast.mediafile_no_dimensions: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: {count} <MediaFile> element(s) are missing width and/or height attributes. VAST §3.8 — players use these dimensions to pick the right rendition for the slot; without them the choice is guesswork and you can end up serving a 1920×1080 file into a 320×180 slot.
vast.skip_offset_invalid: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: <Linear> has an invalid skipoffset value "{val}". Allowed formats: HH:MM:SS or HH:MM:SS.mmm (e.g. 00:00:05) or a percentage 0%–100% (e.g. 20%). Anything else — bare seconds, "15s", or out-of-range percent — is ignored by compliant players and the skip button never appears.
vast.tracking_events_missing: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: <InLine> with <Linear> has no <TrackingEvents>. Quartile beacons (start, firstQuartile, midpoint, thirdQuartile, complete) are how the publisher and SSP verify delivery — without them you lose viewability signals and reconciliation gets painful.
vast.duration_invalid: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: <Linear> Duration value "{val}" is not a valid VAST timecode. Required format: HH:MM:SS or HH:MM:SS.mmm (minutes and seconds 00–59, e.g. "00:00:30" or "00:01:30.500"). An invalid duration causes unpredictable ad timing — players may not know when to fire quartile beacons or allow skipping.
vast.mediafile_type_invalid: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: <MediaFile> declares unsupported MIME type "{type}". Players rely on this value to select a playable rendition; use a VAST-compatible type such as video/mp4, video/webm, application/x-mpegURL, or application/dash+xml.
vast.universaladid_missing: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: VAST 4.x InLine ad is missing <UniversalAdId>. This identifier lets buyers, verification vendors, and players recognise the creative across systems — required by VAST 4.0+.
vast.videoclicks_no_clickthrough: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: VAST InLine has <VideoClicks> with <ClickTracking> but no <ClickThrough> landing URL. The click pixel fires but the user goes nowhere — clicks are unattributable to a campaign landing page and the bid's CPC/CPA value is lost.
vast.nonlinear_no_dimensions: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: {count} <NonLinear> overlay(s) are missing width or height attributes. VAST §3.10 — the player uses these to position and scale the overlay over the video frame. Without them, placement is guesswork and the overlay may not render at all.
response.30.envelope_required: oRTB 3.0 BidResponse is missing the openrtb envelope. 3.0 wraps the response as { openrtb: { ver, response: {...} } }. Without this envelope the exchange can't even tell what protocol version this is.
response.30.ver_required: openrtb.ver is missing in BidResponse. 3.0 requires an explicit version (e.g. "3.0").
response.30.ver_invalid: openrtb.ver = "{ver}" in BidResponse is not 3.x. The envelope only accepts versions starting with "3.".
response.30.response_required: openrtb.response object is missing. The 3.0 envelope has two children: ver + response. Without response there's nothing to consume.
response.30.id_required: openrtb.response.id is missing. Same role as 2.x BidResponse.id — ties the response to the originating BidRequest.
response.30.seatbid_or_nbr_required: openrtb.response has neither seatbid[] nor nbr. Spec requires one of: an array of seat bids OR a no-bid reason code. The exchange will treat this as malformed and drop the response.
response.30.no_bid: No-bid 3.0 response (nbr={nbr}, seatbid empty/missing). Canonical "thanks for the request, not bidding" shape. Not an error.
response.30.seatbid_empty_no_nbr: openrtb.response.seatbid is an empty array AND nbr is absent. Signal a no-bid via the nbr code, not by an empty seatbid.
response.30.seatbid.empty: Seat #{num}: bid[] is empty. Either remove the seatbid entry or include at least one bid.
response.30.bid.invalid: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: not an object. Each bid must be { id, item, price, ... }.
response.30.bid.id_required: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: bid.id is missing. Used for billing reconciliation; required.
response.30.bid.item_required: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: bid.item is missing. 3.0 uses bid.item (string) to reference the request's item.id (replaces 2.x bid.impid). Without it the auction can't tell which placement this bid is for.
response.30.bid.price_required: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: bid.price is missing or not a number. The auction needs a price to clear.
response.30.deep_validation_limited: Note: oRTB 3.0 BidResponse deep validation (AdCOM creative specs in bid.media, advertiser domains) is active.
response.30.bid.media_missing: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: bid.media is missing. oRTB 3.0 / AdCOM requires a media object containing the creative specification.
response.30.bid.media_invalid: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: bid.media must be a valid JSON object.
response.30.bid.media.format_required: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: bid.media has no formats. At least one format of display, video, audio, or native must be defined.
response.30.bid.display_invalid: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: bid.media.display must be a valid JSON object.
response.30.bid.display.markup_required: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: bid.media.display requires adm (HTML markup) or curl (creative URL).
response.30.bid.video_invalid: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: bid.media.video must be a valid JSON object.
response.30.bid.video.markup_required: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: bid.media.video requires adm (VAST XML markup) or curl (VAST URL).
response.30.bid.audio_invalid: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: bid.media.audio must be a valid JSON object.
response.30.bid.audio.markup_required: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: bid.media.audio requires adm (audio markup) or curl (creative URL).
response.30.bid.native_invalid: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: bid.media.native must be a valid JSON object.
response.30.bid.adomain_missing: Seatbid #{sNum} → bid #{bNum}: bid.media.adomain is missing or not a non-empty array of strings. Advertiser domains are recommended for transparency filtering.
mirror.error.input_not_object: Mirror cannot build a pair: input is not a JSON object.
mirror.error.type_not_supported: Mirror currently supports only oRTB 2.x BidRequest or BidResponse. Detected type: {type}.
mirror.note.ortb_30_not_supported: This is an oRTB 3.0 envelope. Mirror v0 covers 2.5/2.6 only — 3.0 needs deep AdCOM validation (next chapter on the roadmap).
mirror.note.cur_inferred_from_request: Currency taken from the request: cur = {cur}.
mirror.note.cur_multi_dropped: Request listed multiple currencies; mirror can only emit one. Picked {picked}, dropped: {dropped}.
mirror.note.cur_invalid_fallback: Request currency "{raw}" is not a valid ISO 4217 code; mirror falls back to {fallback}.
mirror.note.cur_inferred_from_response: Currency taken from the response: cur = {cur}.
mirror.note.banner_size_copied: Slot #{num}: banner size copied from the request — {w}×{h}.
mirror.note.banner_size_default: Slot #{num}: no banner size in the request — used the 300×250 default.
mirror.note.video_vast_emitted: Slot #{num}: emitted a VAST 4.0 InLine template with duration capped to video.maxduration.
mirror.note.audio_vast_emitted: Slot #{num}: emitted a VAST template for audio.
mirror.note.native_adm_emitted: Slot #{num}: built a native adm from the declared request assets (title/img/data with matching ids).
mirror.note.native_unparseable_fallback: Slot #{num}: native.request could not be parsed — fell back to a banner placeholder.
mirror.note.imp_format_unknown_banner_fa: Slot #{num}: format could not be determined — used the 300×250 banner fallback.
mirror.note.bid_price_above_floor: Bid #{num}: price set to {price} (0.10 above floor {floor}).
mirror.note.no_imps_no_bids: Request has no imp[] — returned an empty seatbid.
mirror.note.imp_video_inferred_from_vast: Slot {num}: VAST detected in adm — emitted imp.video with protocols 3,7 and MP4.
mirror.note.imp_native_inferred_from_adm: Slot {num}: native detected in adm — emitted imp.native with the same asset ids.
mirror.note.imp_banner_size_from_bid: Slot {num}: banner size {w}×{h} taken from bid.w/bid.h.
mirror.note.imp_banner_default_size: Slot {num}: response had no size — used the 300×250 banner default.
mirror.note.no_bids_default_banner_imp: Response has no bids — created one default 300×250 banner imp.
mirror.note.bestpractice_response_enrich: Best-practice mode: added crid/cid/cattax/lurl/nurl + DSA bidext (EU compliance) + bidid and seat.
mirror.note.bestpractice_request_enriche: Best-practice mode: added source.ext.schain (sellers.json), regs.coppa/gdpr, user.ext.consent placeholder, device.sua (Structured UA for the post-Chrome-UA-freeze world).
device.client_hints.sua_missing: device.sua (Structured User Agent) is missing. Chrome/Edge 100+ freeze the legacy UA string — without sua the DSP can't target by OS version, browser minor version, or device model. Bid is valid; targeting will be coarser, expect lower buyout.
device.client_hints.os_unknown: OS not specified — neither device.os/.osv nor device.sua.platform carries it. The DSP can't run OS-segmented campaigns (mobile/desktop split, iOS-only creatives, etc.). Add device.os + device.osv, or better — device.sua.platform from client-hints.
device.client_hints.browser_unknown: Browser identity unreachable: no device.browser, no device.sua.browsers[], and device.ua is empty. The DSP can't exclude obsolete browsers or pick creatives that depend on modern WebAPIs. At minimum fill device.ua; ideally device.sua.browsers[] from client-hints.
imp.secure_recommended: Slot #{num}: secure flag is missing or 0. Most publishers serve HTTPS pages; mixed-content creatives (HTTP) get silently blocked by the browser after the bid wins. Setting secure: 1 is the safe default.
imp.secure_invalid: Slot #{num}: secure must be 0 or 1 (numeric flag). It's another type now, so the auction can't reliably determine whether HTTPS is required.
crosscheck.bid.no_floor_set: Slot bidfloor missing — the auction has no explicit minimum, so any non-negative bid clears "the floor" automatically. Spec-valid (oRTB §3.2.4 makes bidfloor optional), but every active SSP exposes it; explicit floors prevent low-CPM races.
imp.pop.fcap_missing: Slot #{num} is a pop-family slot but no frequency cap is set on imp.ext (any of frequency_cap / fcap / freq / cap). Pops are intrusive — without a per-user / per-session cap SSPs typically cut the floor in half and fill drops accordingly. Add a numeric cap so the SSP can budget the slot.
imp.pop.battr_popup_blocked: Slot #{num} is a pop slot but imp.banner.battr blocks pop (attribute 8, IAB List 5.3). If a slot is configured for pop traffic, blocking creative attribute 8 is contradictory and will prevent pop ads from filling.
imp.pop.instl_conflict: Slot #{num} has imp.instl=1 (interstitial) on a pop slot. Interstitials are full-screen overlays, whereas pops are opened in a separate window/tab. This combination is contradictory.
imp.pop.secure_may_block_landing: Slot #{num} is a pop slot with secure:1. Pops historically open HTTP landing pages from a JS handler — mixed-content policy may block the navigation after the bid wins. If your landing is HTTPS this is fine; otherwise consider secure:0.
bid.pop.adm_not_redirect: Seat #{sNum} bid #{bNum}: bid.adm does not look like a pop creative. Pops ship a window.open(URL) script, a top.location redirect, or a bare landing URL — banner HTML in adm won't render as a pop and the impression is lost after the bid wins. Replace with a redirect/script payload that opens the landing.
crosscheck.bid.pop.adomain_landing_misma: Seat #{sNum} bid #{bNum}: popunder adm opens landing host "{landing}" but bid.adomain declares {declared}. Adomain MUST match the host the user actually lands on — mismatch is either a mis-declared advertiser or a spoof. The publisher's anti-phishing/brand-safety filter cannot verify a pop landing in advance, so the declared adomain is the only signal it can trust.
crosscheck.bid.pop.adomain_landing_match: Seat #{sNum} bid #{bNum}: popunder adm landing host "{host}" matches the declared bid.adomain.
feed.clickunder.nobid: Clickunder feed response carries result.status="NOBID" — the upstream auction had no winning bid for this slot. Valid shape; nothing to render.
dialects.question.unknown_ext_signal: Unknown vendor-extension signal at `{path}` with value `{value}`. Review the payload shape and save the appropriate mapping to your dialect.
request.url.user_ip_ipv6: user_ip is IPv6 ({ip}). Many DSPs/SSPs only resolve IPv4 geo data — confirm downstream geo enrichment handles v6 before relying on it.
request.url.ch_field_empty: Client Hints field `{field}` is present but empty. Per Sec-CH-UA spec, omit the parameter for 'unknown'; empty values are a serialization bug some validators reject.
request.url.ch_uafull_quoted: ch-uafull value {value} carries literal quotes. Sec-CH-UA-Full-Version is a bare token — quotes are usually a client-side bug and a few exchanges reject the request outright.
request.url.url_trailing_questionmark: `url=` value ends with a trailing `?`. The empty query suffix is ambiguous and some click-redirect chains drop everything past it — URL-encode the referrer or strip the `?`.
request.url.decode_failed: URL request decoder failed to produce a canonical shape. Either the URL is malformed or no vendor decoder claims this host/path yet.
feed.linkfeed.url_required: Link-feed click URL for bid #{num} is missing — without it the user has nowhere to land on click.
feed.linkfeed.bid_required: result.link[{num}].bid is required (positive float). This bid amount is interpreted by the SSP as the eCPM paid for the impression.
feed.linkfeed.bid_nonpositive: result.link[{num}].bid = {bid} — non-positive bid will be filtered by the SSP. Expected a positive float.
feed.linkfeed.seat_missing: result.link[{num}].seat is absent. The vendor usually echoes the buyer/seat id here — its absence makes server-side attribution harder but the bid itself stays valid.
err-schain-version: source.ext.schain.ver is missing or not "1.0". The SupplyChain spec requires ver="1.0" — exchanges use this to choose the correct parser. Set ver: "1.0".
err-schain-complete: schain.complete must be 0 (incomplete chain) or 1 (complete chain). Missing or non-integer values cause chain parsing to fail on the exchange side.
err-schain-nodes-empty: schain.nodes is missing or empty. A SupplyChain must contain at least one node representing the direct seller.
err-schain-node-asi: schain node[{idx}].asi is missing or not a valid domain (got: "{val}"). asi is the canonical domain of the SSP or exchange and is required for audit trail integrity.
err-schain-node-sid: schain node[{idx}].sid is missing or empty. sid is the seller account ID on the asi domain — required for buyers to verify the seller via ads.txt.
err-schain-node-hp: schain node[{idx}].hp must be 0 or 1 (got: "{val}"). hp=1 means this node is included in the payment flow; hp=0 means it is a passthrough. Missing hp breaks payment attribution.
warn-schain-node-rid-missing: schain node[{idx}].rid is absent. rid (request ID) is optional per spec but strongly recommended — without it buyers cannot correlate impressions back to exchange logs for discrepancy resolution.
warn-schain-node-domain-missing: schain node[{idx}].domain is absent. domain is optional but recommended — it lets buyers cross-reference the seller domain independently of the asi field.
err-eids-not-array: user.ext.eids is present but not an array (got: {type}). The EID spec requires an array of identity source objects — wrap your EID object in an array.
err-eids-source-missing: user.ext.eids[{idx}].source is missing or empty. source must be the canonical domain of the identity provider (e.g. "liveramp.com", "id5-sync.com").
err-eids-uids-empty: user.ext.eids[{idx}].uids is missing or empty. Each EID entry must contain at least one uid record with the actual identifier.
err-eids-uid-id-missing: user.ext.eids[{eidIdx}].uids[{uidIdx}].id is missing or empty. id is the actual user identifier string — without it the EID entry carries no signal.
err-podseq-invalid: podseq = "{val}" is invalid. podseq must be a non-negative integer (0 = any position, 1 = first, 2 = second…).
err-pod-len-mismatch: minduration ({min}s) > maxduration ({max}s). Minimum ad duration cannot exceed the maximum — no bid can satisfy this constraint and the exchange will receive zero bids.
err-pod-len-invalid: {field} = "{val}" is not a positive integer. Ad duration lengths (minduration, maxduration, poddur, maxseq) must be whole positive numbers.
err-schain-node-domain-invalid: schain node[{idx}].domain = "{val}" is not a valid domain. domain must be a bare hostname (e.g. "openx.com") — no scheme, no path. Fix or remove the domain field.
err-schain-node-rid-invalid: schain node[{idx}].rid = "{val}" is not a string. rid must be a non-empty string (the exchange request ID). Convert it to string or remove the field.
err-eids-source-invalid: user.ext.eids[{idx}].source = "{val}" is not a valid domain. EID source must be the canonical domain of the identity provider (e.g. "id5-sync.com"). A bare label without a TLD is rejected.
err-bid-currency-invalid: Currency "{val}" is not valid ISO-4217 ({context}). Currency must be exactly 3 uppercase ASCII letters (e.g. "USD", "EUR", "UAH"). Lowercase or non-standard codes will be misrouted.
err-bid-currency-mismatch: Currency "{val}" is not in the request-allowed set {allowed}. The exchange will settle only in one of the request currencies. Set the bid or response cur to a value in {allowed}.
err-bid-price-negative: bid.price = {val} is not a positive number (seatbid[{si}].bid[{bi}]). bid.price must be a finite number > 0 representing the CPM in the request currency. A zero or negative price will be rejected by the exchange.
err-bid-price-below-floor: bid.price {price} < imp.bidfloor {floor} for impid "{impid}" (seatbid[{si}].bid[{bi}]). The exchange will filter this bid. Raise the price to at least {floor}.
err-tmax-invalid: req.tmax = "{val}" is not a positive integer. tmax must be a whole number of milliseconds > 0. Non-integer or non-positive values cause unpredictable timeout behaviour on the exchange.
warn-tmax-too-small: req.tmax = {val}ms is below {min}ms. At this latency budget almost no DSP will respond in time — the auction will likely time out with no valid bids.
warn-tmax-too-large: req.tmax = {val}ms exceeds {max}ms. Very long timeouts increase auction latency for publishers and are unusual in programmatic RTB. Verify this is intentional.
err-podid-invalid: podid = "{val}" is invalid. podid must be a non-empty string or a positive integer identifying the ad pod group. Fix or remove the podid field.
err-schain-invalid: schain value at {path} is not a plain object (type: {type}). The SChain object must be a JSON object — not a string, array, or null.
err-schain-node-invalid: schain.nodes[{idx}] is not a plain object (type: {type}). Every node in the SupplyChain nodes array must be a JSON object.
err-eids-entry-invalid: {path}[{idx}] is not a plain object (type: {type}). Every entry in the EIDs array must be a JSON object with source and uids fields.
err-eids-uid-invalid: uid at index {uidIdx} in eid[{eidIdx}] is not a plain object (type: {type}). Each uid must be a JSON object with an id field.
err-eids-source-invalid-type: eid[{idx}].source has wrong type (got {type}, expected string). source must be a string domain name (e.g. "liveramp.com").
err-eids-uid-id-invalid-type: uid[{uidIdx}] in eid[{eidIdx}].id has wrong type (got {type}, expected string). id must be a non-empty string containing the actual user identifier.
err-eids-uid-atype-invalid: user.ext.eids[{eidIdx}].uids[{uidIdx}].atype = {val} is outside the allowed range. Per spec atype must be 1 (cookie), 2 (device), or 3 (people-based). Unrecognised atypes cause the EID to be ignored or rejected by DSPs.
warn-currency-conversion-needed: bid currency {bidCur} differs from floor currency {floorCur} for impid "{impid}" (seatbid[{si}].bid[{bi}]). Cross-currency floor comparison requires a rate lookup — Spyglass skips the numeric check. Ensure your exchange converts correctly or use matching currencies.
payload.ambiguous_both_sides: Payload carries BOTH imp[] (request side) and seatbid[] (response side). It was validated as a {validated} only — the other side was ignored. Split the two payloads and validate them separately, or paste them into the request/response panes for a crosscheck.
payload.ambiguous_envelope: Payload mixes an OpenRTB 3.0 envelope (openrtb) with 2.x root fields (imp/seatbid). It was validated as {validated} — the other layer was ignored. Remove the layer that does not belong.
version.assumed: No version-specific fields found — validated against the 2.5 baseline by assumption, not detection. If you target 2.6 or 3.0, pin it via expectedVersion to catch version drift.
version.single_marker: Version 2.6 was detected from a single field ({signal}). One field silently upgraded the whole validation target — if you actually target 2.5, pin expectedVersion to flag it instead.
jsonfeed.not_validated: Recognized as JSON Feed 1.1 by shape (version + items). Structural validation for this format is not implemented — fields were NOT checked.
